residual_risks: PrQueuePage.spec.ts's fourth test ('still shows the generic error state ... for a real failure') mocks fetchPrQueue rejecting with a plain object `{ status: 500 }` rather than a real Error/ApiError instance. Because PrQueuePage.vue's (pre-existing, unchanged) catch block does `e instanceof Error ? e : new Error(String(e))`, this non-Error rejection is rewrapped into `new Error('[object Object]')`, which loses the `.status` field before it ever reaches classifyError -- so the test actually exercises classifyError's generic 'unknown' category, not the 'server' (5xx) category its name/intent implies. This does not weaken coverage of the new LITE-gating logic (the assertions -- role=alert present, retry button present -- correctly prove a real fetchPrQueue failure still routes to the 'error' blockState rather than being swallowed into 'empty'), and 500-to-'server'-category classification is already independently verified in frontend/src/lib/errors.spec.ts (`5xx -> server`) and frontend/src/components/StateBlock.spec.ts. Net effect: cosmetic mismatch between test description and what's technically classified, not a functional gap.

PrQueuePage.vue's new `getJson('/app-config')` call (frontend/src/pages/PrQueue/PrQueuePage.vue:58) duplicates TopBar.vue's existing `/app-config` fetch but bypasses the TanStack Query cache TopBar.vue uses (`useQuery({ queryKey: ['app-config'], queryFn: () => getJson<AppConfig>('/app-config') })`) -- PrQueuePage does a raw, uncached call on every `load()` (including every click of the page's own 刷新 button). Not a test-coverage problem (behavior is deterministic and tested), just a note that the two `/app-config` call sites can diverge in staleness/timing; not exercised by any test because it isn't a behavioral branch.
testing_gaps: No test drives `getJson('/app-config')` itself rejecting (e.g. a network error or non-2xx response fetching the config, as opposed to fetchPrQueue rejecting). It shares the same try/catch as the already-tested fetchPrQueue-failure case (test 4), so the risk of an unguarded regression is low, but the new call site itself is never exercised failing independently.

No test covers the reset/transition case for the new `liteUnavailable` ref: starting from a LITE-unavailable ('empty') render and then calling `load()` again (e.g. via the page's 刷新 button or StateBlock's @retry) with `lite_edition: false` on the second response, to confirm `liteUnavailable.value = false` (set at the top of `load()`, new in this diff) actually flips `blockState`/`emptyText` back to normal rendering rather than getting stuck showing the LITE copy.

No test covers `config.lite_edition` being absent/undefined in the `/app-config` payload (vs. an explicit `false`) -- the code relies on JS truthiness (`if (config.lite_edition)`), which is almost certainly fine, but the assumption is untested.
